Name: Video-linked Thai/Swedish child data corpus
Description: Longitudinal recordings of child-caregiver interaction in everyday contexts. The CLARIN family record describes 60 transcripts from six children in Sweden and Thailand, with videos linked to transcripts on an utterance-by-utterance basis.
CountryOrInstitution: Sweden; Thailand; Lund University Humanities Lab
Notes: Included because Swedish is one of the corpus languages and the resource is already represented in the CLARIN multimodal corpus family.

Name: Svensk teckensprakskorpus / Swedish Sign Language Corpus (STS-korpus)
Description: Web-based corpus tool for searching Swedish Sign Language corpus material. The information page states that video recordings and annotations were produced in the Swedish Sign Language Corpus project and can be downloaded for research and teaching; annotations are intended for use with ELAN.
Infrastructure: Stockholm University; Department of Linguistics; STS-korpus
Notes: The site is in Swedish. Licence details were not stated in the inspected public page; access appears to require registration for some downloads.

Name: RixVox-v2
Description: Swedish parliamentary speech dataset from the National Library of Sweden / KBLab. The Hugging Face record describes audio and text data built by matching and force-aligning parliamentary protocols to media recordings of debates.
Infrastructure: Hugging Face; National Library of Sweden / KBLab
CountryOrInstitution: Sweden; National Library of Sweden / KBLab
Notes: Merged with the earlier RixVox record as the same underlying parliamentary-speech resource family; v2 is listed as the current, larger dataset.

Name: SweDia 2000
Description: Website for listening to recordings of more than 100 Swedish dialects recorded across Sweden and Swedish-speaking parts of Finland. It is included as a Swedish-hosted speech resource with audio linked to dialect/location context.
Infrastructure: University of Gothenburg and Swedish dialect research context
Notes: The landing page clearly exposes audio listening and dialect metadata, but the first-pass inspection did not verify downloadable corpus files or systematic transcription availability.

Name: FLEURS Swedish (sv_se)
Description: Swedish subset of Google's FLEURS multilingual speech dataset. The Hugging Face dataset card lists audio and text modalities, Swedish (sv_se) as one subset, and fields for audio, transcription and raw transcription.
Notes: Included because Swedish is an explicit language subset; not Sweden-hosted.

Name: Mozilla Common Voice Swedish
Description: Crowdsourced speech corpus pairing voice recordings with prompt text and validation metadata. The older Hugging Face record for Common Voice 17.0 states that Common Voice datasets moved to Mozilla Data Collective in October 2025.
Infrastructure: Mozilla Common Voice; Mozilla Data Collective
Notes: Included as a well-known Swedish speech/text resource, but current download and licence details should be rechecked in Mozilla Data Collective because the old Hugging Face dataset record is now empty.

Name: MuMiN multilingual multimodal misinformation dataset
Description: Large-scale multilingual multimodal fact-checked misinformation dataset structured as a heterogeneous graph. The project page describes tweets, users, Twitter threads and fact-checked claims in 41 languages, with source code for compiling/exporting the dataset.
Notes: Potential Swedish-language inclusion is plausible under the 41-language coverage but was not verified in the first-pass source inspection. Retained as a low-confidence lead because it is genuinely multimodal and multilingual.
